Home / Password Managers / Alternatives to 1Password

3 open source alternatives to 1Password

Popular consumer and team password manager. Here are the open source projects real teams use instead — ranked by fit, with honest pros and cons for each.

What people don't love about 1Password

  • Subscription-only since 7.x — no standalone license.
  • Cloud-only by default.
  • Family sharing options have changed repeatedly.

Current 1Password pricing (for reference): Individual from $2.99/month; Family from $4.99/month.

Ad slot — above comparison

Quick comparison

Alternative Best for License Self-host Hosted cloud?
Bitwarden
Open source password manager with free cloud and self-host options. 		The strongest 1Password-like cloud and self-host story. GPL-3.0 / AGPL-3.0 ★★☆☆☆ Yes
Vaultwarden
Lightweight Bitwarden-compatible server in Rust. 		Lightweight self-hosted server that works with Bitwarden clients. AGPL-3.0 ★☆☆☆☆ Self-host only
KeePassXC
Offline password database — KeePass-compatible. 		Offline, no-account password vaults for power users. GPL-3.0 ★☆☆☆☆ Self-host only

1. Bitwarden — The strongest 1Password-like cloud and self-host story.

Open source password manager with free cloud and self-host options.

Strengths

  • Generous free tier on the hosted service.
  • Strong open source credentials.
  • Cross-platform clients and browser extensions.

Weaknesses

  • Self-hosting the full stack is multi-container.
  • Some polish features (password health, reports) are paid.
  • Occasional syncing edge cases on mobile.
License: GPL-3.0 / AGPL-3.0 Self-host difficulty: 2/5 Hosted cloud optionDesktop: Windows, macOS, LinuxMobile: iOS, Android

Bitwarden homepage · Source on GitHub · 1Password vs Bitwarden →

2. Vaultwarden — Lightweight self-hosted server that works with Bitwarden clients.

Lightweight Bitwarden-compatible server in Rust.

Strengths

  • Single container — runs on a Raspberry Pi.
  • Fully compatible with all Bitwarden clients.
  • Trivial backups.

Weaknesses

  • Not officially supported by Bitwarden Inc.
  • Paid Bitwarden features require self-bypass.
  • Home-lab focus — not aimed at large teams.
License: AGPL-3.0 Self-host difficulty: 1/5

Vaultwarden homepage · Source on GitHub · 1Password vs Vaultwarden →

3. KeePassXC — Offline, no-account password vaults for power users.

Offline password database — KeePass-compatible.

Strengths

  • No server — just a single encrypted file you sync yourself.
  • Fast and minimal resource usage.
  • Strong browser integration.

Weaknesses

  • No native mobile app (KeePassDX/Strongbox fill the gap).
  • Sharing between users is manual.
  • Sync is user's responsibility.
License: GPL-3.0 Self-host difficulty: 1/5 Desktop: Windows, macOS, Linux

KeePassXC homepage · Source on GitHub · 1Password vs KeePassXC →
